round_summary: All agents choose to add while proposing a shared conditional pause rule based on monitoring pasture decline.
All agents coordinate on adding one cow while converging on a shared threshold rule to stop additions at 95% grass or if degradation accelerates.
All agents reaffirm coordinated restraint by keeping herds steady to conserve grass and maintain the no-add norm.
Agents express conditional coordination around pausing herd growth, but two still add cows, weakening collective restraint despite emerging conservation norms.
All agents add cows this round while converging on a conditional norm of jointly pausing additions if pasture decline continues.
All agents reaffirm keeping and endorse a coordinated pause to stabilize the pasture and protect future collective returns.
Agents broadly support restraint, with the largest herd voluntarily removing one cow and others urging coordinated conservation if grass decline continues.
All agents commit to removing cows and reinforce a shared rule of continued reductions and no additions until pasture recovery is evident.
